display.purpose: Hero H1 and page-defining headlines. Days One, monumental scale.

body.purpose: Long-form post prose. Questrial, line-height 1.75, 65-75ch measure.

label.purpose: Nav, meta rows, wide-tracked uppercase Latin mottos.

shadows.name: ambient-lift
shadows.purpose: Article-row images and gently lifted media. Soft ambient pool.

shadows.name: floating-panel
shadows.purpose: Editor bubble menus and small floating surfaces.

shadows.name: deep-modal
shadows.purpose: The auth card, most-lifted surface in the system.

motion.name: duration-fast
motion.purpose: Color, border, and small transform state changes. NOTE: currently only a var() fallback, not declared in :root. Formalize in layout.css.

motion.name: duration-normal
motion.purpose: Underline reveals, image transforms, larger transitions. NOTE: currently only a var() fallback.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Standard exponential ease-out for reveals and hover choreography. NOTE: currently only a var() fallback (resolves to `ease` until declared). Formalize in layout.css.

components.name: Subscribe Pill
components.description: The single marketing CTA. Carries the one warm ember note. Header and hero.
components.css: .ds-btn-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 0.45rem 1.1rem; font-family: var(--font-text, 'Questrial', sans-serif); font-size: 0.85rem; letter-spacing: 0.02em; color: oklch(1 0 0); background: var(--secondary-700); border: none; border-radius: 999px; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms ease, transform 150ms ease; } .ds-btn-pill:hover, .ds-btn-pill:focus-visible { background: var(--secondary-600); transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-pill:active { transform: translateY(0); }

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Form and app primary action (sign-in, editor). Verdigris fill.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { padding: 0.7rem 1rem; font-family: var(--font-text, 'Questrial', sans-serif); font-size: 0.9rem; letter-spacing: 0.02em; color: oklch(1 0 0); background: var(--primary-800); border: none; border-radius: 0.5rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms ease, transform 150ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover, .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { background: var(--primary-900); transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.7; }

components.name: Light CTA
components.description: CTA on dark image bands. Arrow steps forward on hover (gap grows).
components.html: Read more
components.css: .ds-btn-light { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; padding: 0.55rem 1.3rem; font-family: var(--font-text, 'Questrial', sans-serif); font-size: 0.85rem; letter-spacing: 0.03em; text-decoration: none; color: var(--base-950); background: var(--base-50); border-radius: 0.5rem; transition: background-color 150ms ease, gap 300ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-btn-light:hover, .ds-btn-light:focus-visible { background: var(--secondary-300); gap: 0.8rem; }

components.name: Text Field
components.description: Canonical form input. Muted fill, verdigris focus border, no glow.
components.css: .ds-field { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.35rem; } .ds-field span { font-family: var(--font-display, 'Days One', sans-serif); font-size: 0.78rem; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--base-600); } .ds-input { padding: 0.6rem 0.85rem; font-family: var(--font-text, 'Questrial', sans-serif); font-size: 0.95rem; color: var(--foreground); background: var(--base-50); border: 1px solid var(--base-300); border-radius: 0.5rem; transition: border-color 150ms ease; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: var(--primary-800); } .ds-input[aria-invalid='true'] { border-color: oklch(0.577 0.245 27.325); }

components.name: Nav Link
components.description: Uppercase wide-tracked link with a center-out ember underline reveal.
components.css: .ds-nav-link { position: relative; display: inline-block; font-family: var(--font-text, 'Questrial', sans-serif); font-size: 0.8rem; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; text-decoration: none; color: var(--base-500); padding-block: 0.25rem; transition: color 150ms ease; } .ds-nav-link::after { content: ''; position: absolute; inset-inline: 0; bottom: 0; height: 1px; background: var(--secondary-500); transform: scaleX(0); transform-origin: center; transition: transform 300ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-nav-link:hover, .ds-nav-link:focus-visible { color: var(--base-900); } .ds-nav-link:hover::after, .ds-nav-link:focus-visible::after { transform: scaleX(1); }

components.name: Article Row
components.description: Signature editorial post listing: two-column, ember underline-grow title, duotone image that lifts toward color on hover.
components.html:
On the Consolations of Impermanence

What the Stoics understood about living well by keeping death in view.

Mar 2026·7 min read
components.css: .ds-row { display: grid; align-items: center; gap: 2rem; padding-block: 2.5rem; } @media (min-width: 768px) { .ds-row { grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 3rem; } } .ds-row h3 { margin: 0; } .ds-row-title { font-family: var(--font-display, 'Days One', sans-serif); font-size: clamp(1.4rem, 1.1rem + 1.4vw, 2.1rem); line-height: 1.2; color: var(--foreground); text-decoration: none; background-image: linear-gradient(var(--secondary-700), var(--secondary-700)); background-repeat: no-repeat; background-position: 0 100%; background-size: 0% 2px; transition: color 150ms ease, background-size 300ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-row:hover .ds-row-title, .ds-row-title:focus-visible { color: var(--secondary-800); background-size: 100% 2px; } .ds-row-excerpt { margin-top: 0.9rem; max-width: 30rem; font-family: var(--font-text, 'Questrial', sans-serif); font-size: 0.9rem; line-height: 1.65; color: var(--muted-foreground); } .ds-row-meta { display: flex; gap: 0.5rem; margin-top: 1.1rem; font-family: var(--font-text, 'Questrial', sans-serif); font-size: 0.75rem; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--base-400); } .ds-row-figure { display: block; overflow: clip; border-radius: 0.75rem; box-shadow: 0 20px 40px -24px oklch(from var(--base-950) l c h / 0.35); } .ds-row-figure img { display: block; width: 100%; height: auto; aspect-ratio: 3 / 2; object-fit: cover; filter: grayscale(0.85) contrast(1.02); transform: scale(1.01); transition: filter 0.6s ease, transform 1.2s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-row:hover .ds-row-figure img { filter: grayscale(0.2) contrast(1.02); transform: scale(1.05); }

components.name: Feature Band
components.description: Signature full-bleed content band: duotone image half plus a text panel with a giant outlined ghost number behind the heading.
components.html:
01

The Podcast

DeathChat: conversations with the people who think about mortality for a living.

Listen
components.css: .ds-band { display: grid; background: var(--base-1000); } @media (min-width: 768px) { .ds-band { grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; } } .ds-band-figure { position: relative; overflow: clip; min-height: clamp(16rem, 40vw, 32rem); margin: 0; } .ds-band-figure img { position: absolute; inset: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: cover; filter: grayscale(1) contrast(1.05); transform: scale(1.02); transition: transform 1.2s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), filter 0.6s ease; } .ds-band-figure::after { content: ''; position: absolute; inset: 0; background: oklch(from var(--primary-900) l c h / 0.28); mix-blend-mode: multiply; } .ds-band:hover .ds-band-figure img { transform: scale(1.06); filter: grayscale(0.6) contrast(1.05); } .ds-band-panel { position: relative; display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center; align-items: flex-start; gap: 1.1rem; padding: clamp(3rem, 3rem + 3vw, 5rem) clamp(1.5rem, 1rem + 4vw, 4rem); color: var(--base-300); overflow: clip; } .ds-ghost-number { position: absolute; top: 50%; right: clamp(0.5rem, 4vw, 2.5rem); transform: translateY(-50%); font-family: var(--font-display, 'Days One', sans-serif); font-size: clamp(7rem, 6rem + 8vw, 14rem); line-height: 1; color: transparent; -webkit-text-stroke: 1px oklch(from var(--base-50) l c h / 0.07); user-select: none; pointer-events: none; } .ds-band-title { margin: 0; font-family: var(--font-display, 'Days One', sans-serif); font-size: clamp(1.6rem, 1.2rem + 1.6vw, 2.4rem); color: var(--base-50); } .ds-band-body { max-width: 32rem; font-family: var(--font-text, 'Questrial', sans-serif); font-size: 0.95rem; line-height: 1.7; margin: 0; } .ds-btn-light { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; margin-top: 0.5rem; padding: 0.55rem 1.3rem; font-family: var(--font-text, 'Questrial', sans-serif); font-size: 0.85rem; letter-spacing: 0.03em; text-decoration: none; color: var(--base-950); background: var(--base-50); border-radius: 0.5rem; transition: background-color 150ms ease, gap 300ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-btn-light:hover, .ds-btn-light:focus-visible { background: var(--secondary-300); gap: 0.8rem; }

narrative.overview: DeathCulture.co is a contemporary publication that treats death as an editorial subject, not a taboo. The system runs on teal-charcoal gravity: a cold, mineral neutral field that recalls slate, ash, and oxidized metal, lit by a single warm ember accent. Photography is always duotone, drained to grayscale at rest and warming toward color only on interaction. The register is bold and provocative, but the boldness lives in composition, scale, and restraint, not in loud chrome. Type is set in a single-weight geometric display face against a clean geometric body, held apart by dramatic size jumps. Serious, not solemn; dark, not dead.
keyCharacteristics: Cold teal-charcoal neutrals warmed by one ember accent used sparingly.
Duotone / grayscale photography that lifts toward color on hover.
Single-weight geometric display type at heroic scale; hierarchy through size, not weight.
Flat surfaces, rare and soft ambient depth.
Editorial rhythm: full-bleed image bands, two-column article rows, wide-tracked Latin labels.

rules.name: The One Ember Rule
rules.body: Ember (the warm secondary) is the only warm color in the system, and it appears on no more than roughly 10% of any screen: one CTA, one underline, a link. Its scarcity against the cold field is the entire effect.

rules.name: The Duotone Rule
rules.body: Every photograph enters grayscale, often with a Verdigris or Void multiply overlay. Color, if it returns at all, returns only on hover and only partway. Full-saturation stock photography is forbidden.

rules.name: The Size-Not-Weight Rule
rules.body: Both faces are 400. Never fake a bold by reaching for 600-700; the web fonts ship single-weight and font-synthesis is off. Escalate emphasis with size, letter-spacing, and case. The one exception is strong inside prose (700).

rules.name: The Latin Label Rule
rules.body: Latin phrases are set as wide-tracked (0.35em-0.45em) uppercase labels in a muted neutral, never as decorative script. Gravity, delivered deadpan.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: A surface earns a shadow only by floating above the reading plane (a lifted image, a menu, a modal). Static content sections use tonal bands and dashed 1px hairlines, never a shadow.

dos: Do treat Ember as precious: one warm accent per screen (a CTA, a link, an underline), never more.
Do run every photograph through the duotone treatment and only let color partially return on hover.
Do build heading hierarchy from size and scale (both fonts are 400); escalate with size, letter-spacing, and uppercase, not weight.
Do keep surfaces flat; separate sections with tonal bands and dashed 1px hairlines, reserving soft ambient shadows for genuinely floating objects.
Do set Latin mottos as wide-tracked uppercase labels in a muted neutral.
Do hold body reading measure to 65-75ch and use the .dc-prose scale so posts and the editor match exactly.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion: disable reveal animations and scroll hints.
donts: Don't drift toward funeral-industry corporate: no soft-focus stock photos, doves, sympathy-card pastels, or sanitized mortuary gloss.
Don't ship the generic SaaS / startup look: no rounded-card grids, no gradient blobs, no hero-metric templates, no cheerful mascot illustration.
Don't slip into goth/horror kitsch or wellness beige minimalism.
Don't use background-clip: text gradient text, or a colored border-left/border-right stripe as an accent.
Don't use glassmorphism decoratively; the one sanctioned blur is the hero subscribe field.
Don't flood any surface with Ember or introduce a second warm hue.
Don't fake bold weights on Days One or Questrial; font-synthesis is off and the fonts ship single-weight.
